title: Microservice Dependency Cascade Failure Across Order Pipeline
symptoms: Inventory service returning 503 errors with connection pool exhaustion
Order service threads blocked waiting for inventory responses
Payment service connection pool exhausted from blocked order requests
API gateway timeout rate at 85% for all order-related endpoints
No circuit breakers tripping despite cascading failures
root_cause: Database connection pool leak in inventory service combined with absence of circuit breakers in the order pipeline, allowing a single service failure to cascade through all dependent services
resolution: Restarted inventory service to clear leaked connections, implemented emergency circuit breakers on critical service boundaries, and scaled up affected services to handle recovery traffic
preventive_actions: Implement circuit breakers on all inter-service communication boundaries
Add connection pool monitoring and leak detection for all services
Deploy service mesh with automatic retry and timeout policies
Create chaos engineering program to test cascade failure scenarios
Implement bulkhead pattern to isolate critical service dependencies

timeline.event: Order service threads begin blocking on inventory calls
Payment service connection pool exhausted from blocked order requests
API gateway timeout rate spikes to 85%
P1 alert fires for order pipeline failure
Root cause traced to inventory service connection pool leak
Inventory service restarted, emergency circuit breakers deployed
Services recovering, order pipeline processing resuming

embedding_summary: Cascade failure across order pipeline triggered by database connection pool leak in inventory service. Absence of circuit breakers allowed failure to propagate to order, payment, and API gateway services. 42-minute outage affecting 28,000 users with $450K revenue loss. Resolved by service restart and emergency circuit breaker deployment.
